rows.source_ref: Robopip, "Forex Mechanical System: SMA Crossover Pullback", babypips.com Art of Automation blog, 2015-06-05, based on Big Pippin's Daily Chart Art framework. URL: https://www.babypips.com/blogs/art-of-automation/forex-system-20150605
rows.concept: Trade in the direction of a 100/200 SMA crossover, triggering on the first stochastic (14,3,3) exit from oversold/overbought as a trend-continuation pullback entry.
rows.eligibility_reason: Fully mechanizable in pure MQL5 (SMA + Stochastic, deterministic entries, fixed 150/300-pip SL/TP + breakeven); EURUSD H1 data available via .DWX; FTMO/DXZ compatible.
rows.notes: Exit: 150-pip stop, 300-pip target (2:1 RR), move stop to entry once price moves 150 pips in favor. Illustrated on EURUSD H1 but applies to FX majors. Generic dual-SMA trend-follow + stochastic-pullback variant with no structural limit-to-arbitrage; source presents NO backtest evidence ('let's see how it fares over the next few months first' - proposal stage). Aligns with prior shop findings that classic indicator/trend-follow FX systems lack structural edge (FX-Edge-Doktrin). Complete rules keep it ELIGIBLE but low-priority.

notes: Single strategy defined on page 3; pages 4-8 are partner ads and blog boilerplate; page 9 empty.
